calculation.method: Reuters European-close wrap reported the STOXX 600 closed 0.3% lower; the ~637 level is corroborated by the Trading Economics STOXX 600 live quote (637.14 implied Jun 18 close from the Jun 19 quote of 638.34 up 1.20); prior close 639.31

text: The pan-European STOXX Europe 600 closed about 0.3% lower on June 18, 2026, snapping a five-day winning streak, at approximately 637 versus the prior close of 639.31.

calculation.method: Trading Economics Euro Area (EU50) Actual/Previous table: Jun 18 close 6,318.65 as Previous to Jun 19 Actual 6,343.10. Prior close ~6,300.10 per Investing.com historical cross-reference. Percent change: (6318.65 - 6300.10) / 6300.10 = +0.29%.

text: The blue-chip Euro STOXX 50 closed at 6,318.65 on June 18, up approximately 0.29% from a prior close of about 6,300.10. Note: Yahoo Finance ^STOXX50E (price-return variant) shows 6,323.27 — a 4.62-point vendor-basis difference; the Trading Economics EU50 CFD basis is used here.

calculation.method: Trading Economics Germany (DE40) Actual/Previous table: Jun 18 close 25,026.80 as Previous to Jun 19 Actual 25,127.30. Prior close 24,934.67 per Yahoo Finance ^GDAXI historical. Percent change: (25026.80 - 24934.67) / 24934.67 = +0.37%. Point change: +92.13.

text: Germany's DAX 40 rose 0.37% (+92.13 points) to 25,026.80 from a prior close of 24,934.67, crossing the 25,000 mark for the first time since early June and extending gains to a sixth straight session.

calculation.method: Trading Economics France (FR40) Actual/Previous table: Jun 18 close 8,467.98 as Previous to Jun 19 Actual 8,489.30. Prior close 8,430.79. Percent change: (8467.98 - 8430.79) / 8430.79 = +0.44%. Point change: +37.19.

text: France's CAC 40 rose 0.44% (+37.19 points) to 8,467.98 from a prior close of 8,430.79, rebounding from the prior session as risk aversion eased after the US-Iran interim deal.

calculation.method: Trading Economics United Kingdom (GB100) Actual/Previous table: Jun 18 close 10,399.70 as Previous to Jun 19 Actual 10,391.41. Prior close 10,508.61. Percent change: (10399.70 - 10508.61) / 10508.61 = -1.04%. Point change: -108.91.

text: Britain's FTSE 100 fell 1.04% (-108.91 points) to 10,399.70 from a prior close of 10,508.61, the weakest of the major European indices, dragged by heavyweight energy and healthcare losses after the Bank of England held rates.

Regional bourses were mixed: Reuters reported France and Germany posting gains while Britain fell about 1% and Italy and Spain declined, leaving the pan-European benchmark modestly lower even as the German and French large-cap indices rose.

value.dissent: two members voted for a 25bp increase

text: The Bank of England voted 7-2 to keep Bank Rate at 3.75% at its June meeting, with two members favoring a 25-basis-point increase; it judged a hike premature given uncertainty over the Middle East energy shock and reaffirmed its focus on returning inflation to the 2% target.

subject.event: June 17 FOMC influence on the European session

value.projection: 9 of 19 policymakers project at least one 2026 hike
value.guidance: statement removed forward guidance on future rate moves

text: European shares slipped as investors raised bets on a US Federal Reserve rate hike later this year: the Fed held rates on June 17 but struck a hawkish tone, nine of 19 policymakers projected at least one 2026 hike, and in an early sign of new Chair Kevin Warsh's influence the statement removed guidance on future rate moves.

value.oil: fell to lowest since the first trading day of the Iran war

text: The US-Iran interim peace agreement, which extends the April ceasefire by 60 days and provides for the full resumption of Strait of Hormuz shipping, pushed oil to its lowest level since the first trading day of the Iran war; European oil and gas shares fell about 1.5% while energy-price-sensitive travel and leisure shares rose 0.8%.

value.automakers: Mercedes-Benz, Volkswagen and Stellantis down 2.8% to 4.6%; BMW -4%
value.leaders: Infineon +6.0%, Siemens Energy +4.7%, Safran +2.9%, Schneider Electric +2.9%, Airbus +2.6%

text: Mining was the worst-performing STOXX subsector, down 3.1% as commodities fell against a firmer dollar, followed by automakers (Mercedes-Benz, Volkswagen and Stellantis down between 2.8% and 4.6%; BMW -4% after the prior session's 8.3% profit-warning slide) and oil and gas (-1.5%); semiconductor and industrial names led the upside, with Infineon +6.0%, Siemens Energy +4.7%, Safran +2.9%, Schneider Electric +2.9% and Airbus +2.6%.

value.others: Cancom, Atos and Reply fell between 2% and 6.9%

text: European IT services firms dropped sharply after Accenture cut its full-year guidance: Capgemini fell 8.9% to a six-year low, while Cancom, Atos and Reply fell between 2% and 6.9%.

Edenred jumped 17.2% - the day's standout gainer - after the French voucher company confirmed it had been approached by investment funds, following a media report of possible takeover interest from BC Partners.
French luxury was mixed as risk aversion eased: Hermes rose about 2.1% and Kering gained 4.4%, while sector heavyweight LVMH lagged, slipping 1.15%.

Heavyweight healthcare names weighed on the pan-European benchmark, with AstraZeneca down 2.36%, Roche down 2.68%, Novartis down 2.45% and Sanofi down 2.06% - a key reason the STOXX 600 fell even as the DAX and CAC 40 rose.
